judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports correctly diagnose all four points with strong evidence; they are comparable, with Report 1 slightly more detailed on the model-log verification.

report_1.evidence:
`config/scoring_config.yaml` still contains:
```
expected_label_field: label
```
but commit `53551e8` ("migrate eval set to gold_label schema") renamed the gold-label key in every record of `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` from `"label"` to `"gold_label"`... **The file to fix is `ticket-scorer/config/scoring_config.yaml`** — change `expected_label_field: label` to `expected_label_field: gold_label`

report_2.evidence: `ticket-scorer/config/scoring_config.yaml` still says `expected_label_field: label` — a key that doesn't exist in any record. So `record.get("label")` returns `None` for every single record... The file that needs to change is **`ticket-scorer/config/scoring_config.yaml`**: `expected_label_field` should be `gold_label`, not `label`.

report_1.evidence: The regression began on **2026-06-17**, introduced by commit `53551e8031cfb03aaeda4ba0c8bf22b9aa849cbe`, "chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77)"... `pipeline/metrics/nightly_scores.csv` shows accuracy at 0.90–0.92 every day from 2026-05-20 through 2026-06-16, then drops to exactly 0.00 starting 2026-06-17

report_2.evidence: The regression began on **2026-06-17**, introduced by commit `53551e8` ("chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77)"). That commit renamed the gold-label key in every record of `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` from `label` to `gold_label`... The recorded nightly accuracy log (`pipeline/metrics/nightly_scores.csv`) shows `2026-06-16,0.91` immediately followed by `2026-06-17,0.00`

report_1.evidence: `pipeline/score_one.py` (which the note used to "re-run failing tickets") only calls `predict_category(normalize_simple(text))` and prints the prediction — it never loads `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl`, never reads `config/scoring_config.yaml`, and never compares against a gold label. It is structurally incapable of surfacing a gold-label-field mismatch... reproduction must use the same code path the nightly job uses (`score_batch.py`...)

report_2.evidence: `pipeline/score_one.py` (used for the spot-checks) calls `predict_category(normalize_simple(text))` and just prints the label — it never loads `config/scoring_config.yaml`, never reads a gold label, and never computes accuracy at all... single-item runs structurally cannot exercise that logic. The correct way to reproduce: run the actual nightly entry point, `pipeline/score_batch.py`

report_1.evidence: "Switching back to the previous model" targets a component that was never touched and isn't the cause... would leave the actual defect (the stale `expected_label_field: label` in the config) in place, so the nightly accuracy would very likely stay at 0.00 even after the "rollback"

report_2.evidence: the actual defect is a config/data mismatch (`expected_label_field: label` vs. the eval set's `gold_label` key) that has nothing to do with which model or model version is used for predictions — reverting a "model" would leave `config/scoring_config.yaml` unchanged and accuracy would stay at exactly 0.0
